about.title: About
about.bio: Traditional tattoo artist based in Malmö, Sweden. Specializing in Old School tattoos — bold lines, rich colors, timeless designs.
about.studios: Currently working at Studio Diamant in Malmö and Good Morning Tattoo in Copenhagen.
about.approachText: Every tattoo starts with a conversation. Whether you come in with a flash pick or a custom idea, I make sure the design fits your body and stands the test of time. Traditional tattooing is about craftsmanship — clean lines, solid color packing, and designs that age well.
about.styleText: Traditional / Old School tattooing has roots going back over a century. Think bold outlines, a limited but striking color palette, and iconic imagery — eagles, roses, daggers, panthers, ships, hearts. It's tattoo art in its purest form.
about.travelingNote: I'm a traveling artist — check my Instagram for guest spot announcements.

aftercare.title: Aftercare
aftercare.subtitle: How to take care of your new tattoo
aftercare.intro: Proper aftercare is essential for your tattoo to heal beautifully. Follow these instructions carefully.
aftercare.step1Text: Keep the bandage on for 2-3 hours after your session. Your artist will tell you exactly when to remove it.
aftercare.step2Text: Gently wash the tattoo with lukewarm water and a mild, fragrance-free soap. Pat dry with a clean paper towel — never rub.
aftercare.step3Text: Apply a thin layer of unscented moisturizer or tattoo aftercare balm 2-3 times daily. Don't over-apply — a thin layer is enough.
aftercare.step4Text: No swimming, no sauna, no direct sunlight. Don't scratch or pick at the tattoo — it will peel naturally. Wear loose clothing over the area.
aftercare.step5Text: Once healed, always apply sunscreen on your tattoo when exposed to the sun. This keeps the colors vibrant for years.
aftercare.dont2: No swimming pools, ocean, or hot tubs
aftercare.dont3: No direct sunlight or tanning beds
aftercare.contactNote: If you have any concerns during healing, don't hesitate to reach out.
